Falaj Capital PJSC (the Company), an ADX-listed issuer registered in Abu Dhabi, issues this UAE PDPL Privacy Notice and MNPI Handling Addendum for employees, advisors, and vendors.
Personal data processed: name, contact details, Emirates ID number (collected but truncated), trade licence details for vendor onboarding, nationality and family-origin where required by KYC, and DIFC/ADGM registry or court filing references where you interact with those authorities.
Purpose and legal bases: contract performance, compliance with SCA/ADX disclosure obligations, insider list maintenance under DFSA/FSRA Market Abuse provisions, and legitimate interests in deal workflow execution.
MNPI controls: information is non-public until disclosed via ADXNet or DFM eDisclosure and the Company website; selective disclosure is prohibited except under confidentiality with delayed disclosure approvals.
Blackout windows: directors, executives, and Designated Insiders (including Mr. Sami Al Barq and Dr. Leila Nasser) must not trade during pre-close periods or while in possession of issuer-material non-public information.
Materiality is assessed relative to the issuer pursuant to SCA Decision No. (3/R.M) of 2020 and ADX Disclosure and Transparency Standards; immaterial operational updates do not constitute inside information.
Example public reference: the Q4 trading update published on ADX on 15 January 2025 and the broad press release on warehouse automation are already public and are not MNPI.
Example non-MNPI operational note: internal office seating changes and an outsourced helpdesk rota change are not issuer-level disclosures.
Transfers: we may transfer personal data and MNPI to processors in ADGM and DIFC and, where necessary, to third countries under PDPL-compliant contractual clauses and FSRA/DFSA MAR confidentiality standards.
Retention: Emirates ID copies are retained in redacted form for 3 years post offboarding; insider list entries for 5 years from the end of the relevant transaction; trade licence records for 7 years to meet audit requirements.
Data subject rights: access, correction, erasure, restriction, and objection may be exercised under PDPL by submitting a DSAR to dpo@falajcapital.example.ae or to PD Officer, Falaj Capital PJSC, PO Box 00000, Abu Dhabi.
Verification: we may request limited identity verification (e.g., last three digits of Emirates ID and trade licence entity name) and will not request full copies by email.
Disclosure timing: market-sensitive announcements will be made without undue delay via ADXNet; any delay requires SCA-compliant justification and monitoring to prevent selective disclosure.
Complaints: you may contact the UAE Data Office or relevant free zone regulator (DFSA/FSRA) after contacting us; we will respond within 30 days under PDPL timeframes.
Note on stale information: references in third-party blogs to a 2023 pilot project are outdated and should not be treated as current issuer guidance.
Contact for transactions: deal-room access requests and wall-crossing confirmations should be sent to compliance@falajcapital.example.ae; access is denied during blackout unless pre-cleared.
